title: Ancient humans had a mining factory in the mountains 7,000 years ago, for 5,000 years
description: Archaeologists have found a human settlement in Spain that sits at 7,333ft and was frequented by ancient humans for 5,000 years. They likely used it for a specific purpose. However, Baishiya Karst Cave is 10,760 feet above sea level and was inhabited for over 100,000 years.

title: Mathematics, Ancient Greek, Biology – Panhellenic exams 2026: Today’s exam topics (3/6)
description: Protothema, in collaboration with the tutoring center “Diakrotima,” presents the exam topics The post Mathematics, Ancient Greek, Biology – Panhellenic exams 2026: Today’s exam topics (3/6) appeared first on ProtoThema English .

title: Scientists Find Yeast in Ancient Iceman's Guts -- and Make Bread
description: Yeast has been growing in the guts of a frozen mummy called Oetzi the Iceman for thousands of years, scientists have discovered, telling AFP they used it to make a tasty sourdough bread. More than 5,300 years ago -- before the Egyptian pyramids were built -- Oezti was strolling through the Alps on the border of Austria and Italy when he was killed by an arrow in the back. He remained frozen in the ice until two German hikers stumbled across his mummified remains in 1991 in the northern Italian region of South Tyrol.

title: Clean energy helped save EU €59 billion in fossil fuel costs in 2025
description: Europe’s growing investment in renewable energy helped the European Union avoid an estimated €59 billion in fossil fuel import costs in 2025, according to new research examining the continent’s energy transition.

title: From Santa Marta to Bonn: Why the fossil fuel transition debate must move from ambition to implementation
description: As climate negotiators gather for the Bonn Climate Change Conference, the global conversation on transitioning away from fossil fuels faces a crucial test. While political declarations and ambitious pledges have become commonplace, the real challenge lies in translating them into practical pathways that account for economic realities, energy security concerns and the developmental needs of [...] The post From Santa Marta to Bonn: Why the fossil fuel transition debate must move from ambition to implementation appeared first on ANC .
